content_of_post_to_comment:
Hey everyone. I’m really struggling to learn through reading. Every time I open a textbook or a work document, I lose focus almost immediately. I get hit with this overwhelming feeling of boredom, my mind drifts, and I realize I’ve "read" three pages without absorbing a single word.

I think my attention span might be fried from screen time, but I genuinely need to learn this material.

How do you force your brain to engage with dry, dense information?

Are there any active reading techniques or note-taking methods that actually keep you awake?

Open to any advice or routine changes. Thanks!
